sources.title: The Reckless Moment (1949)
sources.description: After discovering the dead body of her teenage daughter's lover, a housewife takes desperate measures to protect her family from scandal. Watch on RetroFlix:  https://retroflix.org/the-reckless-moment-1949/

sources.title: The Reckless Moment (1949) Joan Bennett, James Mason - Film Noir Full Movie
sources.description: The Reckless Moment is a 1949 American film noir melodrama directed by Max Ophüls, produced by Walter Wanger, with Burnett Guffey as cinematographer. It starred Joan Bennett and James Mason, The film is based on The Blank Wall (1947), a novel written by Elisabeth Sanxay Holding. sources.title: The Reckless Moment 1949 colorized (James Mason)
sources.description: After discovering that her teenage daughter accidentally killed her older lover, Lucia Harper (Joan Bennett) tries to dispose of the body. Another man (Roy Roberts) is in possession of love letters linking the daughter to the dead man, and he sends Martin Donnelly (James Mason) to blackmail Lucia. But as she desperately tries to raise the money, Donnelly starts to fall in love with her. James Mason as Martin Donnelly Joan Bennett as Lucia Harper Geraldine Brooks as Beatrice 'Bea' Harper Henry O'Neill as Tom Harper Shepperd Strudwick as Ted Darby David Bair as David Harper Roy Roberts as Nagel I colorized this using AI software that doesn't always get the colors right, but I think it looks fairly natural. Hopefully a more professional job will be done someday.

sources.title: The Reckless Moment (restored, colorized) (1949, noir, imdb score: 7.1)
sources.description: The Reckless Moment is a 1949 American noir melodrama film directed by Max Ophüls , produced by Walter Wanger , and released by Columbia Pictures with Burnett Guffey as cinematographer . It starred James Mason and Joan Bennett . The film is based on The Blank Wall (1947), a novel written by Elisabeth Sanxay Holding . The 2001 film The Deep End is a remake based on the same source material.

sources.title: The Reckless Moment 1949 ★★★½ CC: Film-noir/Drama James Mason, Joan Bennett [1:22:04]
sources.description: The Reckless Moment is a 1949 American film noir melodrama directed by Max Ophüls, produced by Walter Wanger, It starred James Mason and Joan Bennett. In the charming community of Balboa 50 miles from Los Angeles, middle-class housewife Lucia Harper travels to Los Angeles to meet scoundrel, Ted Darby. To ask him to stop seeing her seventeen year-old daughter. He asks for money to leave Bea, but Lucia refuses to give any. Bea does not believe her mother when told and during the night she sneaks out to the boat garage to meet Ted who admits that Lucia told the truth.   While fighting with Bea, Ted Darby takes a spill that leads to his death. The next morning, Lucia finds the body and assumes that Bea has killed her lover. Desperate to keep her daughter out of jail, She disposes of the body. Meanwhile, Lucia's suspicions that Ted had sleazy friends are confirmed when gangster Martin Donnelly (James Mason) materializes with the intention of extorting cash from the Darby's on behalf of his partner, Nagel who has several letters Bea had written to Ted. Yet when Martin and Lucia grow increasingly close, the scheme starts to splinter.

metadata.Plot: In the charming community of Balboa 50 miles from Los Angeles, middle-class housewife Lucia Harper travels to Los Angeles to meet scoundrel, Ted Darby. Her seventeen year-old daughter Beatrice is in love with Ted. He asks for money to leave Bea, but Lucia refuses to give any. Bea does not believe her mother when told and during the night she sneaks out to the boat garage to meet Ted who admits that Lucia told the truth. Bea pushes him and Ted falls to his on an anchor. The next morning, Lucia finds the body and assumes that Bea has killed her lover. She decides to get rid of the corpse and puts it in her boat and dumps it far from home. When the police find Ted, a stranger, Martin Donnelly, visits Lucia to blackmail her on behalf of his partner, Nagel who has several letters Bea had written to Ted. Donnelly wants $5000 for the letters. The desperate Lucia tries to raise the amount. Martin falls in love with Lucia and tries to help her too. The dangerous Nagel wants to receive the amount at any price.
metadata.Writer: Henry Garson, Robert Soderberg, Mel Dinelli
metadata.Actors: James Mason, Joan Bennett, Geraldine Brooks
